decisions.decision: Wave 4 plans 04-04a and 04-04b serialize despite same wave number
decisions.rationale: Both plans modify src/components/warp_hermes.rs — files_modified intra-wave overlap forces sequential execution per execute-phase workflow safety net
Project config — all plans run sequentially on main working tree; the executor agent itself updates STATE.md/ROADMAP.md

decisions.decision: Module-level #![allow(dead_code)] in src/mocks/* + scoped allows on Wave-1 carry-forward symbols (Personality::label/ALL, PaletteState, ShellSettings)
decisions.rationale: Wave 3 introduced clippy -D warnings; without these allows the gate fails because Wave 4 hasn't wired the symbols yet. Allows are documented with consuming-wave references

decisions.decision: warp_hermes.rs Wave 3 shim retained (imports demo_block_entries, collects Vec<BlockEntry> into Vec<Block>)
decisions.rationale: Bridge that keeps Wave 2/3 builds green without forcing Wave 4 work to land here. Wave 4 (04-04a/b) replaces this with the full Signal<Vec<BlockEntry>> prop-shape refactor

next_action: Run /gsd-execute-phase 4 to resume — orchestrator will discover the remaining 3 plans (04-04a, 04-04b, 04-05) and execute Wave 4 then Wave 5. Wave 4 must serialize (overlap on warp_hermes.rs). Wave 5 has a checkpoint that requires user interaction.
context_notes: Three of six Phase 4 plans complete. Wave 3 wrap-up commits landed (Task 5 + SUMMARY + tracking). Cargo deps, cfg-gated timer, state.rs vocabulary, and mocks module tree are all in place. Wave 4 is the prop-shape refactor that retires the warp_hermes.rs shim — split into 04-04a (BlockEntry-id propagation through markdown/block/block_stream) and 04-04b (input_box/command_palette/status_bar/agent_panel prop changes); they share warp_hermes.rs so they must run sequentially. Wave 5 is the final WarpHermes() integration with a checkpoint task. Three-platform compile gate (cargo check --no-default-features --features {web|desktop|mobile}) is the canonical mid-phase verification cadence inherited from Phase 1. Borrow-then-await discipline (D-06, clippy.toml-enforced) is now binding — every Signal::write() must drop before any .await.
